taskSpec.title: Task
taskSpec.raw:
```json
{
  "title": "Build a Watch E-commerce Website",
  "summary": "Create a complete watch e-commerce website with product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout functionality. The site should showcase luxury and everyday watches with filtering, search, and a responsive design optimized for both desktop and mobile users.",
  "requirements": [
    "Responsive homepage with hero section and featured watches",
    "Product catalog page with filtering by brand, price, style, and features",
    "Individual product detail pages with images, specifications, and add-to-cart",
    "Shopping cart with quantity management and price calculation",
    "Checkout flow with shipping and payment integration",
    "User authentication for account management and order history",
    "Admin dashboard for product and order management",
    "Search functionality with autocomplete",
    "SEO optimization and performance best practices"
  ],
  "steps": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "description": "Set up project structure with Next.js/React, configure TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and establish folder architecture",
      "agent_role": "frontend",
      "files_likely_affected": [
        "package.json",
        "tsconfig.json",
        "tailwind.config.js",
        "next.config.js",
        "src/app/layout.tsx",
        "src/app/page.tsx",
        "src/styles/globals.css",
        ".env.local",
        ".env.example"
      ],
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "description": "Design and implement the database schema for products, users, orders, and cart items",
      "agent_role": "backend",
      "files_likely_affected": [
        "prisma/schema.prisma",
        "src/lib/db.ts",
        "src/types/product.ts",
        "src/types/user.ts",
        "src/types/order.ts",
        "src/types/cart.ts"
      ],
      "depends_on": [1]
    },

      "agent_role": "frontend",

        "src/app/page.tsx",

results.title: Building an E-Commerce Watch Website
results.summary: There are multiple platforms available for building watch e-commerce websites including Shopify, Ecwid, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and Shift4Shop. These platforms offer customizable templates, secure payment processing, and multi-channel selling capabilities. Key considerations include choosing the right niche (luxury, vintage, pre-owned watches), finding reliable suppliers, and implementing trust-building features for high-value purchases.
key_insights: Shopify is used by over 2 million eCommerce sellers globally and is considered one of the most complete e-commerce solutions
You can sell watches from your own store, third-party marketplaces (Amazon, eBay), niche platforms (Mercari, Poshmark), social media, and wholesale channels
Theme selection affects conversion rate, trustworthiness, and online visibility - choose themes aligned with budget and industry needs
Ecwid and other platforms offer no transaction fees and mobile app management for running business on-the-go
Interactive product pages with multiple images, zoom, and 360-degree views are essential for watch sales
Mobile responsiveness is critical for SEO and user experience
Building trust is especially important for luxury/high-priced watch sales - consider third-party authentication services like Watchbox or Chrono24
results.implementation_notes: Steps to build: (1) Choose your niche (new, pre-owned, luxury, casual watches), (2) Partner with reliable wholesale distributors or manufacturers, (3) Select an e-commerce platform (Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, or Magento for larger operations), (4) Choose a watch-appropriate theme with high-quality image support, (5) Set up secure payment processing, (6) Configure shipping and tax automation, (7) Implement marketing via SEO, social media, and product feeds. Consider dropshipping for minimal overhead or wholesale for more control over inventory.

results.query: build a watch website (DIY watchmaking kits context)
results.title: DIY Watch Building Kits and Resources
results.summary: For those interested in physical watchmaking, companies like DIY Watch Club and Rotate Watches offer complete watchmaking kits with all components, tools, and video instructions needed to build mechanical watches. These kits are designed for beginners and have a reported 98% success rate without requiring assistance.
key_insights: DIY Watch Club kits include all components, necessary tools, and video guides tested by over 15,000 first-time watchmakers
Average assembly time is 2-3 hours with structured video instructions broken into small sections
Popular beginner movements include Seiko NH35/NH36, ETA, and ST36 Seagull movements
Essential components: case, dial, movement, hands (plus crystal, bezel insert, winding stem usually included)
Key tools needed: tweezers, screwdrivers, hand pressers, loupe magnifier, and dusty cleaner (Rodico)
AliExpress (Miuksi Official Store) offers cheaper alternative parts organized by movement type
YouTube tutorials are recommended for specific techniques like hand installation and stem cutting
results.implementation_notes: For a DIY watchmaking website/business: (1) Consider offering curated watch kits with pre-assembled movements for beginners, (2) Include comprehensive video tutorials with each step, (3) Offer tool sets separately or as bundle options, (4) Use Seiko NH35/NH36 or similar reliable movements that are widely available and beginner-friendly, (5) Provide customer support for assembly issues, (6) Consider offering assembly services for customers who want custom designs without self-assembly.

knowledgeSummary.executive_summary: Building a watch website can mean either an e-commerce platform for selling watches or a DIY watchmaking kit business. For e-commerce, platforms like Shopify, WooCommerce, or BigCommerce provide turnkey solutions with customizable themes, secure payments, and multi-channel selling. Trust-building features and high-quality product imagery are critical for converting customers on high-value watch purchases.
key_concepts: Niche selection: Define whether you're selling luxury, vintage, pre-owned, casual, or DIY kit watches - this shapes your entire brand and supplier strategy
Multi-channel selling: Modern watch businesses sell across own website, Amazon, eBay, social media, and wholesale simultaneously
Trust signals: High-value purchases require authentication services, secure payments, and professional presentation to convert
Inventory model: Choose between dropshipping (low overhead, less control) or wholesale (more investment, better margins and control)
implementation_guide: Define your niche and target customer (luxury collectors, casual buyers, DIY enthusiasts)
Select e-commerce platform - Shopify for simplicity, WooCommerce for WordPress integration, Magento for enterprise scale
Choose a watch-optimized theme with zoom, 360-degree views, and mobile responsiveness
Establish supplier relationships - wholesale distributors, manufacturers, or dropship partners
Configure secure payment processing (Stripe, PayPal, Shop Pay)
Set up automated shipping calculations and tax compliance
Implement SEO, product feeds for Google Shopping, and social media integration
Add trust elements: SSL, authentication badges, customer reviews, return policies
apis_and_functions: Shopify Storefront API: Headless commerce implementation for custom frontends
WooCommerce REST API: Product management, order processing, inventory sync
Stripe/PayPal SDKs: Secure payment processing integration
Google Merchant Center: Product feed syndication for Shopping ads
Shippo/ShipStation API: Automated shipping label generation and tracking
pitfalls: Poor mobile experience: Over 60% of e-commerce traffic is mobile - test extensively on devices
Low-quality imagery: Watches require macro photography and multiple angles - invest in professional photos
Missing trust signals: High-ticket items need visible security badges, authentication, and clear return policies
Ignoring SEO: Theme selection and page structure directly impact search visibility
No transaction fee awareness: Some platforms charge per-transaction fees that eat into margins on expensive items
code_patterns: Product variant system: Handle multiple SKUs for different watch straps, case colors, movement types
Inventory sync middleware: Keep stock levels consistent across multiple sales channels
Image optimization pipeline: Compress and serve responsive images for fast loading without quality loss
Abandoned cart recovery: Implement email automation for high-value cart abandonment
Wishlist/notification system: Allow customers to track out-of-stock or upcoming watch models

plan.result:
# Implementation Plan: Watch E-commerce Website

## Overview
Building a comprehensive watch e-commerce website with modern tech stack including Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Prisma ORM, and PostgreSQL.

## Step-by-Step Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Project Foundation Setup
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
package.json
tsconfig.json
tailwind.config.js
next.config.js
src/app/layout.tsx
src/app/page.tsx
src/app/globals.css
.env.local
.env.example
.gitignore
README.md
```

**Actions:**
1. Initialize Next.js 14 project with TypeScript
2. Install dependencies: Tailwind CSS, Prisma, NextAuth.js, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod
3. Configure TypeScript with strict mode
4. Set up Tailwind CSS with custom theme for watch website
5. Create environment variable templates
6. Set up basic app layout with metadata and fonts

**Dependencies:** None
**Risk Mitigation:** Use latest stable versions, document all configuration choices

---

### Step 2: Database Schema Design
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
prisma/schema.prisma
src/lib/db.ts
src/types/product.ts
src/types/user.ts
src/types/order.ts
src/types/cart.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Design Prisma schema with tables: User, Product, Category, Brand, Cart, CartItem, Order, OrderItem, Review
2. Set up PostgreSQL connection
3. Create TypeScript interfaces matching database models
4. Add database indexes for performance
5. Set up database seeding for initial data

**Dependencies:** Step 1
**Risk Mitigation:** Use database migrations, create backup strategies, add proper foreign key constraints

---

### Step 3: UI Component Library
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/components/ui/Button.tsx
src/components/ui/Card.tsx
src/components/ui/Modal.tsx
src/components/ui/Input.tsx
src/components/ui/Select.tsx
src/components/ui/Badge.tsx
src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx
src/components/ui/Toast.tsx
src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx
src/components/ui/index.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Create reusable Button component with variants (primary, secondary, outline)
2. Build Card component for product displays
3. Implement Modal component with portal rendering
4. Create form Input components with validation states
5. Build Select dropdown with search functionality
6. Add Badge component for product labels
7. Create Skeleton loaders for performance perception
8. Implement Toast notification system

**Dependencies:** Step 1
**Risk Mitigation:** Use TypeScript for prop validation, implement comprehensive testing

---

### Step 4: Layout Components
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/components/layout/Header.tsx
src/components/layout/Navigation.tsx
src/components/layout/MobileMenu.tsx
src/components/layout/SearchBar.tsx
src/components/layout/CartIcon.tsx
src/components/layout/Footer.tsx
src/app/layout.tsx (modify)
```

**Actions:**
1. Build responsive Header with logo, navigation, search, cart icon
2. Create Navigation component with watch categories
3. Implement mobile-first MobileMenu with slide-out functionality
4. Build SearchBar with autocomplete and search suggestions
5. Create CartIcon with item count badge and mini-cart preview
6. Design Footer with links, social media, newsletter signup

**Dependencies:** Step 3
**Risk Mitigation:** Test across different screen sizes, ensure accessibility compliance

---

### Step 5: Homepage Implementation
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/page.tsx
src/components/home/HeroBanner.tsx
src/components/home/FeaturedCollections.tsx
src/components/home/BestSellers.tsx
src/components/home/BrandShowcase.tsx
src/components/home/Newsletter.tsx
src/components/common/ProductCarousel.tsx
```

**Actions:**
1. Create Hero section with rotating banners for featured watches
2. Build FeaturedCollections showcasing luxury, casual, sports watches
3. Implement BestSellers carousel with top-selling products
4. Add BrandShowcase highlighting premium watch brands
5. Create Newsletter signup with email validation
6. Build reusable ProductCarousel component with navigation controls

**Dependencies:** Step 4
**Risk Mitigation:** Optimize images for web, implement lazy loading, ensure mobile responsiveness

---

### Step 6: Product API Routes
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/api/products/route.ts
src/app/api/products/[id]/route.ts
src/app/api/products/search/route.ts
src/app/api/products/featured/route.ts
src/app/api/categories/route.ts
src/app/api/brands/route.ts
src/lib/services/productService.ts
src/lib/utils/validation.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Create GET /api/products with filtering, sorting, pagination
2. Implement GET /api/products/[id] for individual product details
3. Build search API with fuzzy matching and filters
4. Add featured products endpoint for homepage
5. Create category and brand endpoints
6. Implement productService with database queries
7. Add request validation using Zod schemas

**Dependencies:** Step 2
**Risk Mitigation:** Add rate limiting, input sanitization, error handling, caching strategies

---

### Step 7: Product Catalog Page
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/shop/page.tsx
src/app/shop/layout.tsx
src/components/shop/ProductGrid.tsx
src/components/shop/ProductCard.tsx
src/components/shop/FilterSidebar.tsx
src/components/shop/PriceRangeFilter.tsx
src/components/shop/BrandFilter.tsx
src/components/shop/SortDropdown.tsx
src/components/shop/Pagination.tsx
src/hooks/useProductFilters.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Create shop page with grid layout showing all watches
2. Build ProductCard with image, price, quick view option
3. Implement FilterSidebar with price range, brand, category filters
4. Add sorting options (price, popularity, newest)
5. Create pagination with load more functionality
6. Build custom hook for filter state management
7. Add URL state synchronization for filters

**Dependencies:** Steps 5, 6
**Risk Mitigation:** Implement debounced filtering, optimize API calls, add loading states

---

### Step 8: Product Detail Page
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/shop/[slug]/page.tsx
src/components/product/ImageGallery.tsx
src/components/product/ProductInfo.tsx
src/components/product/ProductSpecs.tsx
src/components/product/AddToCartButton.tsx
src/components/product/QuantitySelector.tsx
src/components/product/ReviewSection.tsx
src/components/product/RelatedProducts.tsx
src/components/product/Breadcrumb.tsx
```

**Actions:**
1. Create dynamic product page with SEO-optimized metadata
2. Build ImageGallery with zoom, thumbnails, 360° view
3. Display product information, pricing, availability
4. Show detailed specifications table
5. Implement AddToCart functionality with size/color variants
6. Add customer reviews and ratings system
7. Show related/recommended products
8. Add breadcrumb navigation

**Dependencies:** Step 7
**Risk Mitigation:** Optimize image loading, handle out-of-stock scenarios, implement error boundaries

---

### Step 9: Authentication System
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts
src/lib/auth.ts
src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx
src/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx
src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx
src/components/auth/RegisterForm.tsx
src/components/auth/SocialLoginButtons.tsx
src/middleware.ts
src/hooks/useAuth.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Configure NextAuth.js with multiple providers (Google, GitHub)
2. Set up email/password authentication with bcrypt
3. Create login/register pages with form validation
4. Implement protected route middleware
5. Add user session management
6. Create authentication hooks and utilities
7. Handle authentication errors and redirects

**Dependencies:** Step 2
**Risk Mitigation:** Implement secure password policies, add CSRF protection, handle authentication failures gracefully

---

### Step 10: Shopping Cart System
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/api/cart/route.ts
src/app/api/cart/items/route.ts
src/app/api/cart/items/[id]/route.ts
src/lib/services/cartService.ts
src/store/cartStore.ts
src/hooks/useCart.ts
src/providers/CartProvider.tsx
src/app/cart/page.tsx
src/components/cart/CartSidebar.tsx
src/components/cart/CartItem.tsx
```

**Actions:**
1. Create cart API routes for CRUD operations
2. Implement cart state management with Zustand
3. Build persistent cart (localStorage + database sync)
4. Create cart page with item management
5. Add cart sidebar for quick view/edit
6. Implement quantity updates and item removal
7. Add cart calculations (subtotal, tax, shipping)

**Dependencies:** Steps 6, 9
**Risk Mitigation:** Handle concurrent cart updates, implement cart expiration, add inventory validation

---

### Step 11: Checkout Process
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/checkout/page.tsx
src/app/checkout/layout.tsx
src/components/checkout/CheckoutForm.tsx
src/components/checkout/ShippingForm.tsx
src/components/checkout/PaymentForm.tsx
src/components/checkout/OrderSummary.tsx
src/app/api/orders/route.ts
src/app/api/payments/stripe/route.ts
src/lib/services/orderService.ts
src/lib/services/paymentService.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Create multi-step checkout flow
2. Implement shipping address form with validation
3. Integrate Stripe for payment processing
4. Add order summary with tax calculation
5. Create order confirmation page
6. Implement order management API
7. Add email notifications for orders

**Dependencies:** Step 10
**Risk Mitigation:** Add payment validation, implement order rollback on failure, secure payment data handling

---

### Step 12: User Dashboard
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/(dashboard)/account/page.tsx
src/app/(dashboard)/account/orders/page.tsx
src/app/(dashboard)/account/profile/page.tsx
src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx
src/components/dashboard/OrderHistory.tsx
src/components/dashboard/ProfileForm.tsx
src/components/dashboard/AddressBook.tsx
src/app/api/users/profile/route.ts
src/app/api/users/orders/route.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Create user dashboard with navigation
2. Build order history with status tracking
3. Implement profile management
4. Add address book functionality
5. Create wishlist feature
6. Add account settings and preferences

**Dependencies:** Steps 9, 11
**Risk Mitigation:** Protect sensitive user data, implement proper authorization, add data validation

---

### Step 13: Admin Panel (Basic)
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/(admin)/admin/page.tsx
src/app/(admin)/admin/products/page.tsx
src/app/(admin)/admin/orders/page.tsx
src/app/(admin)/layout.tsx
src/components/admin/ProductManager.tsx
src/components/admin/OrderManager.tsx
src/app/api/admin/products/route.ts
src/app/api/admin/orders/route.ts
src/middleware.ts (modify)
```

**Actions:**
1. Create admin authentication middleware
2. Build product management interface
3. Implement order management system
4. Add basic analytics dashboard
5. Create bulk product upload functionality
6. Implement inventory management

**Dependencies:** Steps 6, 11
**Risk Mitigation:** Implement role-based access control, add audit logging, secure admin routes

---

### Step 14: Search & SEO Optimization
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
src/app/search/page.tsx
src/components/search/SearchResults.tsx
src/components/search/SearchFilters.tsx
src/lib/services/searchService.ts
src/app/sitemap.xml
src/app/robots.txt
src/lib/utils/seo.ts
```

**Actions:**
1. Implement full-text search with PostgreSQL
2. Add search result page with filters
3. Create SEO-optimized meta tags
4. Generate dynamic sitemap
5. Implement structured data markup
6. Add search analytics

**Dependencies:** Step 7
**Risk Mitigation:** Optimize search performance, handle empty results gracefully, implement search suggestions

---

### Step 15: Performance & Deployment
**Files to Create/Modify:**
```
next.config.js (modify)
src/lib/cache.ts
src/components/common/ImageOptimizer.tsx
Dockerfile
docker-compose.yml
.github/workflows/deploy.yml
vercel.json
```

**Actions:**
1. Implement Redis caching for API responses
2. Optimize images with Next.js Image component
3. Add bundle analyzer and performance monitoring
4. Set up Docker containers
5. Configure CI/CD pipeline
6. Deploy to Vercel/AWS with database
7. Set up monitoring and error tracking

**Dependencies:** All previous steps
**Risk Mitigation:** Implement comprehensive testing, add health checks, set up backup strategies

## Dependencies Summary
- Steps 1-3 can run in parallel after project setup
- Steps 4-5 require UI components (Step 3)
- Steps 6-8 build the core product functionality
- Steps 9-12 handle user features
- Steps 13-15 add admin and optimization features

## Potential Risks & Mitigation Strategies

1. **Performance Issues**: Implement caching, optimize images, use pagination
2. **Security Vulnerabilities**: Regular dependency updates, input validation, secure authentication
3. **Payment Processing Failures**: Implement proper error handling, webhook validation
4. **Mobile Responsiveness**: Mobile-first design, extensive device testing
5. **SEO Performance**: Server-side rendering, proper meta tags, structured data
6. **Inventory Synchronization**: Real-time updates, conflict resolution strategies
7. **High Traffic Handling**: Implement rate limiting, caching, CDN usage

## Tech Stack Summary
- **Frontend**: Next.js 14, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- **Backend**: Next.js API Routes, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL
- **Authentication**: NextAuth.js
- **Payments**: Stripe
- **State Management**: Zustand
- **Deployment**: Vercel/Docker
- **Caching**: Redis
- **Storage**: AWS S3 (images)

### Step 1: build a watch website
I'll help you build a comprehensive watch e-commerce website. Let's start with **Step 1: Project Foundation Setup** as outlined in the implementation plan.

## Step 1: Project Foundation Setup

### 1. Initialize Next.js 14 Project

First, let's create the project structure and configuration files:

**package.json**
```json
{
  "name": "watch-store",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "db:generate": "prisma generate",
    "db:push": "prisma db push",
    "db:studio": "prisma studio",
    "db:seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@next/font": "^14.0.0",
    "@prisma/client": "^5.6.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.0.5",
    "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu": "^2.0.6",
    "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.0.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-toast": "^1.1.5",
    "@stripe/stripe-js": "^2.2.0",
    "bcryptjs": "^2.4.3",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.0",
    "clsx": "^2.0.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.294.0",
    "next": "14.0.3",
    "next-auth": "^4.24.5",
    "react": "^18",
    "react-dom": "^18",
    "react-hook-form": "^7.48.2",
    "stripe": "^14.7.0",
    "tailwind-merge": "^2.0.0",
    "zod": "^3.22.4",
    "zustand": "^4.4.7"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6",
    "@types/node": "^20",
    "@types/react": "^18",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.0.1",
    "eslint": "^8",
    "eslint-config-next": "14.0.3",
    "postcss": "^8",
    "prisma": "^5.6.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.3.0",
    "tsx": "^4.6.0",
    "typescript": "^5"
  }
}
```

**tsconfig.json**
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "es6"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [
      {
        "name": "next"
      }
    ],
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"],
      "@/components/*": ["./src/components/*"],
      "@/lib/*": ["./src/lib/*"],
      "@/types/*": ["./src/types/*"],
      "@/hooks/*": ["./src/hooks/*"],
      "@/store/*": ["./src/store/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```
